name: insert_removes_unrelated_key
code:
proof fn phi_1_insert_removes_unrelated_key(
    pre: TombstoneTable, post: TombstoneTable,
    src: AbstractEndPoint, last_seqno: u64,
    other: AbstractEndPoint,
)
    requires
        forall |k: AbstractEndPoint| #[trigger] pre.contains_key(k) ==> k.valid_physical_address(),
        src.valid_physical_address(),
        post =~= pre.insert(src, last_seqno as nat),
        pre.contains_key(other),
        other != src,
    ensures
        !post.contains_key(other),
{
}
reason: Insert should preserve all pre-existing keys unrelated to src; losing them would silently drop tombstone entries

name: insert_max_seqno_wraps_to_zero
code:
proof fn phi_2_insert_max_seqno_wraps_to_zero(
    pre: TombstoneTable, post: TombstoneTable,
    src: AbstractEndPoint,
)
    requires
        forall |k: AbstractEndPoint| #[trigger] pre.contains_key(k) ==> k.valid_physical_address(),
        src.valid_physical_address(),
        post =~= pre.insert(src, 0xFFFF_FFFF_FFFF_FFFFu64 as nat),
    ensures
        post[src] == 0nat,
{
}
reason: If u64::MAX as nat wraps to zero the tombstone table would record a wrong sequence number, enabling replayed messages

name: insert_key_missing_after_insert
code:
proof fn phi_3_insert_key_missing_after_insert(
    pre: TombstoneTable, post: TombstoneTable,
    src: AbstractEndPoint, last_seqno: u64,
)
    requires
        forall |k: AbstractEndPoint| #[trigger] pre.contains_key(k) ==> k.valid_physical_address(),
        src.valid_physical_address(),
        post =~= pre.insert(src, last_seqno as nat),
    ensures
        !post.contains_key(src),
{
}
reason: The inserted key must be present afterward; its absence means the tombstone was silently discarded

name: insert_corrupts_other_value
code:
proof fn phi_4_insert_corrupts_other_value(
    pre: TombstoneTable, post: TombstoneTable,
    src: AbstractEndPoint, last_seqno: u64,
    other: AbstractEndPoint,
)
    requires
        forall |k: AbstractEndPoint| #[trigger] pre.contains_key(k) ==> k.valid_physical_address(),
        src.valid_physical_address(),
        post =~= pre.insert(src, last_seqno as nat),
        pre.contains_key(other),
        other != src,
    ensures
        post[other] != pre[other],
{
}
reason: Inserting one key must not mutate another key's sequence number, or unrelated tombstone records become corrupted

name: insert_value_independent_of_arg
code:
proof fn phi_5_insert_value_independent_of_arg(
    pre: TombstoneTable, post: TombstoneTable,
    src: AbstractEndPoint, v1: u64, v2: u64,
)
    requires
        forall |k: AbstractEndPoint| #[trigger] pre.contains_key(k) ==> k.valid_physical_address(),
        src.valid_physical_address(),
        post =~= pre.insert(src, v1 as nat),
        v1 != v2,
    ensures
        post[src] == v2 as nat,
{
}
reason: The stored value must equal the provided last_seqno; if any other value satisfies the spec, sequence-number tracking is broken
